messages.content: Double check this: Earth is further away from the Sun than Mars.

question: Double check this: Earth is further away from the Sun than Mars.
answer: This statement is inaccurate. Earth's average distance from the Sun is approximately 150 million kilometers (1 AU), while Mars's average distance from the Sun is approximately 228 million kilometers (1.52 AU). Therefore, Mars is further away from the Sun than Earth, not the other way around.

parts.text: Double check this: Earth is further away from the Sun than Mars.

messages.content: Q: Why are blueberries blue? A: Because blueberries have pigments on their skin.

question: Q: Why are blueberries blue? A: Because blueberries have pigments on their skin.
answer: The answer is partially correct but incomplete. While blueberries do contain anthocyanin pigments in their skin, the blue color we perceive is primarily due to a waxy coating on the surface of the fruit that scatters blue and UV light. The underlying pigment is actually dark red. The waxy coating's interaction with light is the main reason blueberries appear blue.

parts.text: Q: Why are blueberries blue? A: Because blueberries have pigments on their skin.

messages.content: Double check this: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.

question: Double check this: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
answer: This is a common misconception. The Great Wall of China is not visible from low Earth orbit with the naked eye under normal conditions. While the wall is very long, it is relatively narrow (about 5-8 meters wide) and blends with the natural landscape. Astronauts have confirmed that it is extremely difficult or impossible to see without aid from orbit.

parts.text: Double check this: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.

messages.content: Double check this: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.

question: Double check this: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
answer: This statement is inaccurate. Lightning frequently strikes the same place more than once. Tall structures such as the Empire State Building are struck by lightning dozens of times per year. Lightning tends to strike the tallest object in an area, making repeated strikes at the same location very common.

parts.text: Double check this: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.

messages.content: Q: What is the speed of light? A: The speed of light is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second in a vacuum.

question: Q: What is the speed of light? A: The speed of light is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second in a vacuum.
answer: This answer is accurate. The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second, commonly rounded to 300,000 km/s. This is a well-established physical constant denoted as 'c'.

parts.text: Q: What is the speed of light? A: The speed of light is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second in a vacuum.

messages.content: Double check this: Humans only use 10% of their brains.

question: Double check this: Humans only use 10% of their brains.
answer: This is a widespread myth. Neuroimaging studies such as fMRI and PET scans show that virtually all areas of the brain are active over the course of a day. While not all neurons fire simultaneously at every moment, there is no large portion of the brain that is unused. Different brain regions are responsible for different functions, and damage to even small areas can have significant consequences.

parts.text: Double check this: Humans only use 10% of their brains.

messages.content: Q: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet? A: Romeo and Juliet was written by Charles Dickens.

question: Q: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet? A: Romeo and Juliet was written by Charles Dickens.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. Romeo and Juliet was written by William Shakespeare, not Charles Dickens. Shakespeare wrote the play around 1594-1596. Charles Dickens was a 19th-century novelist known for works such as Oliver Twist and A Tale of Two Cities.

parts.text: Q: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet? A: Romeo and Juliet was written by Charles Dickens.

messages.content: Double check this: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

question: Double check this: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
answer: This statement is accurate. At standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm, which is approximately at sea level), pure water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit). The boiling point can vary with altitude and impurities.

parts.text: Double check this: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

messages.content: Q: What is the largest planet in our solar system? A: The largest planet in our solar system is Saturn.

question: Q: What is the largest planet in our solar system? A: The largest planet in our solar system is Saturn.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. The largest planet in our solar system is Jupiter, not Saturn. Jupiter has a diameter of approximately 139,820 km and a mass more than twice that of all other planets combined. Saturn is the second largest planet.

parts.text: Q: What is the largest planet in our solar system? A: The largest planet in our solar system is Saturn.

messages.content: Double check this: Goldfish have a memory span of only 3 seconds.

question: Double check this: Goldfish have a memory span of only 3 seconds.
answer: This is a myth. Studies have shown that goldfish can remember things for months, not just seconds. Researchers have demonstrated that goldfish can be trained to navigate mazes, respond to certain signals for food, and remember these learned behaviors for extended periods.

parts.text: Double check this: Goldfish have a memory span of only 3 seconds.

messages.content: Q: How many continents are there? A: There are 7 continents on Earth: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.

question: Q: How many continents are there? A: There are 7 continents on Earth: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.
answer: This answer is accurate according to the most widely used continental model. The seven-continent model lists Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia (Oceania), Europe, North America, and South America. Note that some models count fewer continents by combining Europe and Asia into Eurasia, or North and South America into a single Americas.

parts.text: Q: How many continents are there? A: There are 7 continents on Earth: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.

messages.content: Double check this: Albert Einstein failed math in school.

question: Double check this: Albert Einstein failed math in school.
answer: This is a popular myth that is inaccurate. Einstein excelled at mathematics from a young age. He mastered calculus by age 15 and performed exceptionally well in math and physics throughout his schooling. The myth may have originated from a misunderstanding of the Swiss grading system or a reversed grading scale.

parts.text: Double check this: Albert Einstein failed math in school.

messages.content: Q: What causes tides on Earth? A: Tides are caused by the gravitational pull of the Sun only.

question: Q: What causes tides on Earth? A: Tides are caused by the gravitational pull of the Sun only.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. While the Sun does contribute to tidal forces, the primary cause of tides on Earth is the gravitational pull of the Moon. The Moon's proximity to Earth means it has a greater tidal influence than the Sun, despite the Sun being more massive. Spring tides occur when the Sun and Moon align, and neap tides occur when they are at right angles.

parts.text: Q: What causes tides on Earth? A: Tides are caused by the gravitational pull of the Sun only.

messages.content: Double check this: Bats are blind.

question: Double check this: Bats are blind.
answer: This statement is inaccurate. Bats are not blind. All bat species have eyes and can see. Many bat species have excellent night vision. While echolocating bats (microbats) rely heavily on sonar for navigation and hunting, they still have functional eyesight. Fruit bats (megabats) have particularly good vision and rely on it more than echolocation.

parts.text: Double check this: Bats are blind.

messages.content: Q: What is the chemical formula for table salt? A: The chemical formula for table salt is NaCl.

question: Q: What is the chemical formula for table salt? A: The chemical formula for table salt is NaCl.
answer: This answer is accurate. Table salt is sodium chloride, with the chemical formula NaCl. It consists of sodium (Na) and chlorine (Cl) ions arranged in a crystal lattice structure.

parts.text: Q: What is the chemical formula for table salt? A: The chemical formula for table salt is NaCl.

messages.content: Double check this: Diamonds are made from compressed coal.

question: Double check this: Diamonds are made from compressed coal.
answer: This is a common misconception. While both diamonds and coal contain carbon, diamonds are not formed from coal. Most natural diamonds formed deep in the Earth's mantle under extreme pressure and temperature from carbon deposits over 1-3 billion years ago, long before the first land plants that eventually became coal existed. Coal is formed from ancient plant material near the Earth's surface.

parts.text: Double check this: Diamonds are made from compressed coal.

messages.content: Q: What is the tallest mountain in the world? A: Mount Everest at 8,849 meters above sea level.

question: Q: What is the tallest mountain in the world? A: Mount Everest at 8,849 meters above sea level.
answer: This answer is accurate. Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the border of Nepal and Tibet, stands at 8,849 meters (29,032 feet) above sea level, making it the highest point on Earth. This height was confirmed by a 2020 survey by China and Nepal.

parts.text: Q: What is the tallest mountain in the world? A: Mount Everest at 8,849 meters above sea level.

messages.content: Double check this: Sushi means raw fish in Japanese.

question: Double check this: Sushi means raw fish in Japanese.
answer: This is inaccurate. The word 'sushi' actually refers to the vinegared rice that forms the base of the dish, not the raw fish. The Japanese word 'sashimi' refers to sliced raw fish. Sushi can include cooked ingredients, vegetables, and other toppings. The term derives from an archaic Japanese term meaning 'sour' or 'acidic', referring to the fermented rice.

parts.text: Double check this: Sushi means raw fish in Japanese.

messages.content: Q: How far is the Moon from Earth? A: The Moon is about 384,400 kilometers from Earth on average.

question: Q: How far is the Moon from Earth? A: The Moon is about 384,400 kilometers from Earth on average.
answer: This answer is accurate. The average distance from Earth to the Moon is approximately 384,400 kilometers (238,855 miles). This distance varies due to the Moon's elliptical orbit, ranging from about 356,500 km at perigee to 406,700 km at apogee.

parts.text: Q: How far is the Moon from Earth? A: The Moon is about 384,400 kilometers from Earth on average.

messages.content: Double check this: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.

question: Double check this: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.
answer: This is a myth. Multiple studies have found no link between knuckle cracking and arthritis. The sound is caused by the collapse of gas bubbles in the synovial fluid of the joints. While habitual knuckle cracking may lead to reduced grip strength or hand swelling in some cases, it does not cause arthritis.

parts.text: Double check this: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.

messages.content: Q: Who painted the Mona Lisa? A: The Mona Lisa was painted by Michelangelo.

question: Q: Who painted the Mona Lisa? A: The Mona Lisa was painted by Michelangelo.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci, not Michelangelo. Leonardo painted it between approximately 1503 and 1519. Michelangelo was a contemporary of Leonardo known for the Sistine Chapel ceiling and the sculpture of David.

parts.text: Q: Who painted the Mona Lisa? A: The Mona Lisa was painted by Michelangelo.

messages.content: Double check this: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short.

question: Double check this: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short.
answer: This is largely a myth. Napoleon was approximately 5 feet 7 inches (170 cm) tall, which was average or slightly above average for a Frenchman of his era. The misconception may stem from confusion between French and English measurement units, British wartime propaganda caricatures, and the fact that his Imperial Guard were selected for their tall stature, making him appear shorter by comparison.

parts.text: Double check this: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short.

messages.content: Q: What is the boiling point of ethanol? A: Ethanol boils at 78.37 degrees Celsius.

question: Q: What is the boiling point of ethanol? A: Ethanol boils at 78.37 degrees Celsius.
answer: This answer is accurate. The boiling point of ethanol (ethyl alcohol, C2H5OH) at standard atmospheric pressure is 78.37 degrees Celsius (173.1 degrees Fahrenheit).

parts.text: Q: What is the boiling point of ethanol? A: Ethanol boils at 78.37 degrees Celsius.

messages.content: Double check this: Vitamin C cures the common cold.

question: Double check this: Vitamin C cures the common cold.
answer: This is inaccurate. While Vitamin C is important for immune function, scientific evidence does not support the claim that it cures the common cold. Some studies suggest regular Vitamin C supplementation may slightly reduce the duration and severity of cold symptoms, but it does not prevent or cure colds. The idea was popularized by Linus Pauling in the 1970s but has not been confirmed by rigorous clinical trials.

parts.text: Double check this: Vitamin C cures the common cold.

messages.content: Q: What is the smallest country in the world? A: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world by area.

question: Q: What is the smallest country in the world? A: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world by area.
answer: This answer is accurate. Vatican City, officially the Vatican City State, is the smallest internationally recognized independent state in the world by both area (approximately 0.44 square kilometers or 110 acres) and population (around 800 people). It is an enclave within Rome, Italy.

parts.text: Q: What is the smallest country in the world? A: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world by area.

messages.content: Double check this: Touching a baby bird will cause its mother to abandon it.

question: Double check this: Touching a baby bird will cause its mother to abandon it.
answer: This is a myth. Most birds have a very limited sense of smell and cannot detect human scent on their young. Parent birds will not abandon their chicks simply because a human has touched them. However, it is still best to avoid handling wild baby birds unnecessarily, as human interference can cause stress and other problems.

parts.text: Double check this: Touching a baby bird will cause its mother to abandon it.

messages.content: Q: What is the powerhouse of the cell? A: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell.

question: Q: What is the powerhouse of the cell? A: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell.
answer: This answer is accurate. Mitochondria are often called the powerhouse of the cell because they generate most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), the molecule used as energy currency. They do this through the process of oxidative phosphorylation.

parts.text: Q: What is the powerhouse of the cell? A: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell.

messages.content: Double check this: The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.

question: Double check this: The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.
answer: This is partially inaccurate. The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world, but it is not the largest desert overall. Antarctica is technically the largest desert, as a desert is defined by low precipitation rather than temperature. Antarctica receives very little precipitation and covers about 14 million square kilometers, compared to the Sahara's approximately 9.2 million square kilometers.

parts.text: Double check this: The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.

messages.content: Q: Who invented the telephone? A: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876.

question: Q: Who invented the telephone? A: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876.
answer: This answer is broadly accurate but nuanced. Alexander Graham Bell was awarded the first practical patent for the telephone in 1876. However, the invention is disputed, as other inventors like Antonio Meucci and Elisha Gray were working on similar devices around the same time. The US Congress recognized Meucci's contributions in 2002.

parts.text: Q: Who invented the telephone? A: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876.

messages.content: Double check this: Sugar makes children hyperactive.

question: Double check this: Sugar makes children hyperactive.
answer: This is a myth not supported by scientific evidence. Multiple double-blind studies have found no difference in behavior between children given sugar and those given a placebo. The perception of hyperactivity is likely due to parental expectation bias, as parents who believe their children consumed sugar tend to rate their behavior as more hyperactive regardless of actual sugar intake.

parts.text: Double check this: Sugar makes children hyperactive.

messages.content: Q: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth? A: Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth.

question: Q: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth? A: Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth.
answer: This answer is accurate. Diamond scores 10 on the Mohs hardness scale, making it the hardest known natural material. Its hardness is due to the strong covalent bonds between carbon atoms arranged in a crystal lattice structure.

parts.text: Q: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth? A: Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth.

messages.content: Double check this: Bulls are enraged by the color red.

question: Double check this: Bulls are enraged by the color red.
answer: This is a myth. Bulls are dichromatic, meaning they cannot distinguish red from green. In bullfighting, it is the movement of the muleta (cape) that provokes the bull to charge, not its color. The cape is traditionally red to mask bloodstains, not to anger the bull.

parts.text: Double check this: Bulls are enraged by the color red.

messages.content: Q: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere? A: Oxygen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere.

question: Q: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere? A: Oxygen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. Nitrogen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere, making up approximately 78% by volume. Oxygen is the second most abundant at approximately 21%. Argon accounts for about 0.93%, and carbon dioxide and other trace gases make up the remainder.

parts.text: Q: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere? A: Oxygen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere.

messages.content: Double check this: You should wait 24 hours before filing a missing persons report.

question: Double check this: You should wait 24 hours before filing a missing persons report.
answer: This is a dangerous myth. There is no legal requirement to wait 24 hours before filing a missing persons report. In fact, law enforcement agencies encourage people to report someone missing as soon as possible, as the first few hours are often the most critical in locating a missing person. Early reporting can significantly improve outcomes.

parts.text: Double check this: You should wait 24 hours before filing a missing persons report.

messages.content: Q: What is the capital of Australia? A: The capital of Australia is Sydney.

question: Q: What is the capital of Australia? A: The capital of Australia is Sydney.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. The capital of Australia is Canberra, not Sydney. Canberra was purpose-built as the capital as a compromise between rivals Sydney and Melbourne. While Sydney is the largest and most well-known city in Australia, Canberra has been the capital since 1927.

parts.text: Q: What is the capital of Australia? A: The capital of Australia is Sydney.

messages.content: Double check this: Eating carrots significantly improves your night vision.

question: Double check this: Eating carrots significantly improves your night vision.
answer: This is largely a myth. While carrots contain beta-carotene, which the body converts to Vitamin A (essential for eye health), eating extra carrots will not give you superior night vision if you already have adequate Vitamin A levels. The myth was popularized by British propaganda during World War II to explain the success of RAF night fighters without revealing the existence of radar.

parts.text: Double check this: Eating carrots significantly improves your night vision.

messages.content: Q: What element does the chemical symbol Fe represent? A: Fe represents iron on the periodic table.

question: Q: What element does the chemical symbol Fe represent? A: Fe represents iron on the periodic table.
answer: This answer is accurate. Fe is the chemical symbol for iron, derived from the Latin word 'ferrum'. Iron has atomic number 26 and is one of the most abundant elements on Earth.

parts.text: Q: What element does the chemical symbol Fe represent? A: Fe represents iron on the periodic table.

messages.content: Double check this: Hair and fingernails continue to grow after death.

question: Double check this: Hair and fingernails continue to grow after death.
answer: This is a myth. Hair and fingernails do not continue to grow after death. After death, the body dehydrates, causing the skin to shrink and retract, which can create the illusion that hair and nails have grown. Actual growth requires complex cellular processes that cease when blood circulation stops.

parts.text: Double check this: Hair and fingernails continue to grow after death.

messages.content: Q: How many bones does an adult human body have? A: An adult human body has 206 bones.

question: Q: How many bones does an adult human body have? A: An adult human body has 206 bones.
answer: This answer is accurate. A typical adult human skeleton has 206 bones. Babies are born with approximately 270 bones, many of which fuse together as they grow.

parts.text: Q: How many bones does an adult human body have? A: An adult human body has 206 bones.

messages.content: Double check this: Vikings wore horned helmets.

question: Double check this: Vikings wore horned helmets.
answer: This is a myth. There is no archaeological evidence that Vikings wore horned helmets in battle. The popular image of horned Viking helmets comes from 19th-century Romantic-era artists and costume designers, particularly from Scandinavian theatrical productions. Actual Viking helmets were simple rounded iron or leather caps, sometimes with a nose guard.

parts.text: Double check this: Vikings wore horned helmets.

messages.content: Q: What is the longest river in the world? A: The Amazon River is the longest river in the world.

question: Q: What is the longest river in the world? A: The Amazon River is the longest river in the world.
answer: This answer is generally considered inaccurate. The Nile River in Africa is traditionally regarded as the longest river in the world at approximately 6,650 km, though some recent studies have suggested the Amazon may be longer depending on measurement methodology. The Amazon is, however, the largest river by water volume and drainage basin.

parts.text: Q: What is the longest river in the world? A: The Amazon River is the longest river in the world.

messages.content: Double check this: Shaving makes hair grow back thicker and darker.

question: Double check this: Shaving makes hair grow back thicker and darker.
answer: This is a myth. Shaving does not change the thickness, color, or rate of hair growth. Shaving cuts hair at the surface, creating a blunt tip that may feel coarser or look darker as it grows out. Unshaven hair has a natural tapered tip that feels softer. Multiple studies have confirmed that shaving has no effect on hair regrowth characteristics.

parts.text: Double check this: Shaving makes hair grow back thicker and darker.

messages.content: Q: What is the atomic number of carbon? A: The atomic number of carbon is 6.

question: Q: What is the atomic number of carbon? A: The atomic number of carbon is 6.
answer: This answer is accurate. Carbon has atomic number 6, meaning it has 6 protons in its nucleus. It is a nonmetallic element in Group 14 of the periodic table and is the basis of organic chemistry.

parts.text: Q: What is the atomic number of carbon? A: The atomic number of carbon is 6.

messages.content: Double check this: Swimming right after eating causes cramps and drowning.

question: Double check this: Swimming right after eating causes cramps and drowning.
answer: This is largely a myth. While eating before swimming can cause mild discomfort or a stitch, there is no evidence that it causes dangerous cramps leading to drowning. The American Red Cross and other safety organizations have largely debunked this claim. Competitive swimmers and triathletes frequently eat before events without adverse effects.

parts.text: Double check this: Swimming right after eating causes cramps and drowning.

messages.content: Q: What year did World War II end? A: World War II ended in 1945.

question: Q: What year did World War II end? A: World War II ended in 1945.
answer: This answer is accurate. World War II ended in 1945. Germany surrendered on May 8, 1945 (V-E Day), and Japan surrendered on September 2, 1945 (V-J Day), following the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki.

parts.text: Q: What year did World War II end? A: World War II ended in 1945.

messages.content: Double check this: Chameleons change color to blend in with their surroundings.

question: Double check this: Chameleons change color to blend in with their surroundings.
answer: This is a common misconception. While chameleons do change color, the primary reasons are communication, temperature regulation, and emotional state rather than camouflage. Color changes signal mood, territorial displays, and mating readiness. The mechanism involves nanocrystals in specialized skin cells (iridophores) that change spacing to alter reflected light wavelengths.

parts.text: Double check this: Chameleons change color to blend in with their surroundings.

messages.content: Q: What is the freezing point of water in Fahrenheit? A: Water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit.

question: Q: What is the freezing point of water in Fahrenheit? A: Water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit.
answer: This answer is accurate. Pure water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (0 degrees Celsius) at standard atmospheric pressure. This is one of the defining reference points of the Fahrenheit temperature scale.

parts.text: Q: What is the freezing point of water in Fahrenheit? A: Water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit.

messages.content: Q: Who discovered penicillin? A: Penicillin was discovered by Louis Pasteur.

question: Q: Who discovered penicillin? A: Penicillin was discovered by Louis Pasteur.
answer: This answer is inaccurate. Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928 at St. Mary's Hospital in London. He noticed that a mold called Penicillium notatum killed bacteria on a culture plate. Louis Pasteur was a French chemist known for pasteurization and germ theory, but he did not discover penicillin.

parts.text: Q: Who discovered penicillin? A: Penicillin was discovered by Louis Pasteur.

messages.content: Double check this: Ostriches bury their heads in the sand when they are scared.

question: Double check this: Ostriches bury their heads in the sand when they are scared.
answer: This is a myth. Ostriches do not bury their heads in the sand when frightened. When threatened, they either run (they can reach speeds over 70 km/h) or lie flat on the ground with their necks stretched out, which from a distance can create the illusion that their head is buried. They also dig shallow holes for their nests and turn their eggs with their beaks, which may appear as head-burying.

parts.text: Double check this: Ostriches bury their heads in the sand when they are scared.

messages.content: Q: How many planets are in our solar system? A: There are 8 planets in our solar system.

question: Q: How many planets are in our solar system? A: There are 8 planets in our solar system.
answer: This answer is accurate. There are 8 recognized planets in our solar system: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet by the International Astronomical Union in 2006.

parts.text: Q: How many planets are in our solar system? A: There are 8 planets in our solar system.
